revision.text:
[[File:Kawahara jinja.jpg|thumb|Kawahara Shrine]] The {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Showa ward of Nagoya, central Japan.
The construction date of the shrine is not clear, it is assumed however that is appeared around 1000. The shrine underwent a number of reconstructions and renovations, the last one being in 1998. There is a pond with turtles that surround the shrine.
Since Buddhism and Shinto were closely intertwined, the shrine was also dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. Therefore it is better known as the "Benten of Kawana" rather than "Kawahara Shrine".
The nearest bus stop is "Bentenmae" on the No. 18.
{{Shinto shrine}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]]
{{shinto-stub}}
[[ja:川原神社]]

[[File:Kawahara jinja.jpg|thumb|Kawahara Shrine]] The {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] and Buddhist [[Benten-dō]] located in the Showa ward of [[Nagoya]], central Japan.
The construction date of the shrine is not clear, it is assumed however that is appeared around 1000. The shrine underwent a number of reconstructions and renovations, the last one being in 1998. There is a pond with turtles that surround the shrine.
Since Buddhism and Shinto were closely intertwined, the shrine was also dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. Therefore it is better known as the "Benten of Kawana" rather than "Kawahara Shrine".
The nearest bus stop is "Bentenmae" on the No. 18.
{{Shinto shrine}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]]
{{shinto-stub}}
[[ja:川原神社]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "Shikinai Shosha" to "Shikinai Shōsha"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata-->
{{Infobox religious building | Name = Kawara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kawaharajinja1.JPG | location = Izumi 1559, Saha-cho, [[Ise]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.15125|N|136.94413889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before 927 CE (Enchō 5) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Kawana-hommachi|WD=Q26160574}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kawara Shrine''' (川原神社, *Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == Ranked 20th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine)<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11</ref>.
This shrine was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, with its enshrined deity considered the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miya River's}} [[Chinjugami|guardian spirit]]<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.70</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.71</ref>.
It is located on the eastern bank of the Miyagawa River<ref name="gk"/>, at 1559 Izumi, Sochi-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.76</ref>. The place name "Sochi" means "marshland," indicating the presence of marshes from the Miyagawa River<ref name="gk"/>. Records also mention rice offerings to the Inner Shrine from Sochi<ref name="sk6">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.346</ref>.
The shrine is referred to as "Kawahara Shrine" in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' and also appears as "Kawara Shrine" or "Kawara-sha" in other records. Locally, it has been called "Senzui" (川水, meaning river water or spring), "Odago," and "Yagura"<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.345</ref>.
== Grounds == The shrine precinct covers an area of 3,219 m²<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.347</ref>. The grounds are serene, with sunlight filtering through trees, accompanied by the sounds of birds and flowing water<ref name="ic"/>. The forest includes species such as {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Chamaecyparis obtusa}}, {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphor tree}}, {{ill|Oak|ja|カシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oak|1=Cyclobalanopsis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q796117}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|en|Machilus thunbergii|WD=Q5792073|lt=Machilus thunbergii}}, and {{ill|Ilex latifolia|en|Ilex latifolia|WD=Q2706373|lt=Ilex latifolia}}<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is '''Mikoto no Mitama of Tsukuyomi''' (月読尊御魂)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity is also worshipped at the {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|1=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|2=ja|3=月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|ja|月夜見宮|1=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|2=ja|3=月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|qq=}}, part of the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine<ref name="tm"/>. Although commonly regarded as a moon deity, the connection between the moon and water is emphasized here, with Tsukuyomi revered as a river deity at Kawahara Shrine<ref name="ic"/>.
Some theories suggest that both Sochi-cho, where Kawahara Shrine is located, and Nakamura-cho, home to Tsukuyomi-no-Miya, share historical ties through the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}<ref name="sk6"/>.
== History == According to legend, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was greeted here by Ōwakako no Mikoto (大若子命) before sailing down the Miyagawa River<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 CE, which defines the auxiliary shrines of Ise<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. According to the ''Daijingū-shiki'', it was one of the shrines maintained by the Guji (宮司), receiving imperial offerings during the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} and {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}}<ref name="sk5"/>.
However, during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, ritual practices ceased, and the shrine fell into disrepair<ref name="tm"/>. The hereditary priests (祝部) also disappeared<ref name="sk6"/>.
In 1663 (Kanbun 3), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} restored the shrine in Sochi<ref name="sk5"/>. A new shrine building was erected on October 9, 1663, and the deity was ceremonially transferred (遷御) on January 10, 1664<ref name="sk5"/>. Descendants of former priests were sought to resume their duties<ref name="sk6"/>. While local traditions guided the shrine's restoration, some suggest that the original site may have been {{ill|Takegahana Shrine|ja|竹鼻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takegahana Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Takegahana-cho or {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}} in Funae<ref name="tm"/>.
Since the deity's transfer in 1694 (Genroku 7), the shrine has remained in its current location<ref name="sk5"/>. The shrine was rebuilt in 1908 (Meiji 41)<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.11</ref>, renovated in 1960 (Shōwa 35), and repaired again in 1983 (Shōwa 58)<ref name="sk6"/>.
== Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}}, and other special ceremonies, priests offer sacred food (御饌) and ritual offerings (幣帛) at the shrine<ref name="sk6"/>. For ceremonies like the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, remote rituals are conducted at the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Inner Shrine<ref name="sk6"/>.
== Access == The shrine is located within a grove just off {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.346–347</ref>.
* **By Bus:** Approximately a 12-minute walk (700 m) from the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū}} "Sochi" bus stop<ref name="ic"/>. From the bus stop, enter the alley opposite {{ill|Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|ja|伊勢市立佐八小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|1=Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379036}}, then follow the path along the rice fields to reach the shrine<ref name="ic"/>. * **By Train:** About 5.5 km from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}}, served by both {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}} {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line}}<ref name="sk5"/>.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. revision.comment: Importing Wikidata [[Wikipedia:Short description|short description]]: "Shinto shrine in Aichi Prefecture, Japan"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Aichi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2025}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} | image = Kawaharajinja2.JPG | caption = Kawahara Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|9|4.5|N|136|56|38.9|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Shōwa-ku]], [[Nagoya]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:Kawaharajinja1.JPG|thumb|''Haiden'' main hall]] The {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] and Buddhist [[Benten-dō]] located in [[Shōwa-ku]], [[Nagoya]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
The construction date of the shrine is not clear, it is assumed however that is appeared around 1000. The shrine underwent a number of reconstructions and renovations, the last one being in 1998. There is a pond with turtles that surround the shrine.
Since Buddhism and Shinto were closely intertwined, the shrine was also dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. Therefore, it is better known as the "Benten of Kawana" rather than "Kawahara Shrine".
The nearest [[Nagoya Municipal Bus]] stop is "Bentenmae" on the No. 18.
{{Shinto shrine}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shōwa-ku]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nagoya]]
{{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata-->
{{Infobox religious building | Name = Kawara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kawaharajinja1.JPG | location = Izumi 1559, Saha-cho, [[Ise]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.15125|N|136.94413889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before 927 CE (Enchō 5) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Kawana-hommachi|WD=Q26160574}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kawara Shrine''' (川原神社, *Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == Ranked 20th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine)<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11</ref>.
This shrine was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, with its enshrined deity considered the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miya River's}} [[Chinjugami|guardian spirit]]<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.70</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.71</ref>.
It is located on the eastern bank of the Miyagawa River<ref name="gk"/>, at 1559 Izumi, Sochi-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.76</ref>. The place name "Sochi" means "marshland," indicating the presence of marshes from the Miyagawa River<ref name="gk"/>. Records also mention rice offerings to the Inner Shrine from Sochi<ref name="sk6">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.346</ref>.
The shrine is referred to as "Kawahara Shrine" in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' and also appears as "Kawara Shrine" or "Kawara-sha" in other records. Locally, it has been called "Senzui" (川水, meaning river water or spring), "Odago," and "Yagura"<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.345</ref>.
== Grounds == The shrine precinct covers an area of 3,219 m²<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.347</ref>. The grounds are serene, with sunlight filtering through trees, accompanied by the sounds of birds and flowing water<ref name="ic"/>. The forest includes species such as {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Chamaecyparis obtusa}}, {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphor tree}}, {{ill|Oak|ja|カシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oak|1=Cyclobalanopsis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q796117}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|en|Machilus thunbergii|WD=Q5792073|lt=Machilus thunbergii}}, and {{ill|Ilex latifolia|en|Ilex latifolia|WD=Q2706373|lt=Ilex latifolia}}<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is '''Mikoto no Mitama of Tsukuyomi''' (月読尊御魂)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity is also worshipped at the {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|1=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|2=ja|3=月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|ja|月夜見宮|1=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|2=ja|3=月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|qq=}}, part of the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine<ref name="tm"/>. Although commonly regarded as a moon deity, the connection between the moon and water is emphasized here, with Tsukuyomi revered as a river deity at Kawahara Shrine<ref name="ic"/>.
Some theories suggest that both Sochi-cho, where Kawahara Shrine is located, and Nakamura-cho, home to Tsukuyomi-no-Miya, share historical ties through the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}<ref name="sk6"/>.
== History == According to legend, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was greeted here by Ōwakako no Mikoto (大若子命) before sailing down the Miyagawa River<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 CE, which defines the auxiliary shrines of Ise<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. According to the ''Daijingū-shiki'', it was one of the shrines maintained by the Guji (宮司), receiving imperial offerings during the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} and {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}}<ref name="sk5"/>.
However, during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, ritual practices ceased, and the shrine fell into disrepair<ref name="tm"/>. The hereditary priests (祝部) also disappeared<ref name="sk6"/>.
In 1663 (Kanbun 3), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} restored the shrine in Sochi<ref name="sk5"/>. A new shrine building was erected on October 9, 1663, and the deity was ceremonially transferred (遷御) on January 10, 1664<ref name="sk5"/>. Descendants of former priests were sought to resume their duties<ref name="sk6"/>. While local traditions guided the shrine's restoration, some suggest that the original site may have been {{ill|Takegahana Shrine|ja|竹鼻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takegahana Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Takegahana-cho or {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}} in Funae<ref name="tm"/>.
Since the deity's transfer in 1694 (Genroku 7), the shrine has remained in its current location<ref name="sk5"/>. The shrine was rebuilt in 1908 (Meiji 41)<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.11</ref>, renovated in 1960 (Shōwa 35), and repaired again in 1983 (Shōwa 58)<ref name="sk6"/>.
== Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}}, and other special ceremonies, priests offer sacred food (御饌) and ritual offerings (幣帛) at the shrine<ref name="sk6"/>. For ceremonies like the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, remote rituals are conducted at the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Inner Shrine<ref name="sk6"/>.
== Access == The shrine is located within a grove just off {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.346–347</ref>.
* **By Bus:** Approximately a 12-minute walk (700 m) from the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū}} "Sochi" bus stop<ref name="ic"/>. From the bus stop, enter the alley opposite {{ill|Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|ja|伊勢市立佐八小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|1=Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379036}}, then follow the path along the rice fields to reach the shrine<ref name="ic"/>. * **By Train:** About 5.5 km from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}}, served by both {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}} {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line}}<ref name="sk5"/>.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[河原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 伊勢神宮{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（外宮）摂社。 * [[宮本村 (三重県)]]

'''Kawara Shrine''' (川原神社, *Kawara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == Ranked 20th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine)<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11</ref>.
This shrine was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, with its enshrined deity considered the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miya River's}} [[Chinjugami|guardian spirit]]<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.70</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.71</ref>.
It is located on the eastern bank of the Miyagawa River<ref name="gk"/>, at 1559 Izumi, Sochi-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.76</ref>. The place name "Sochi" means "marshland," indicating the presence of marshes from the Miyagawa River<ref name="gk"/>. Records also mention rice offerings to the Inner Shrine from Sochi<ref name="sk6">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.346</ref>.
The shrine is referred to as "Kawahara Shrine" in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' and also appears as "Kawara Shrine" or "Kawara-sha" in other records. Locally, it has been called "Senzui" (川水, meaning river water or spring), "Odago," and "Yagura"<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.345</ref>.
== Grounds == The shrine precinct covers an area of 3,219 m²<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.347</ref>. The grounds are serene, with sunlight filtering through trees, accompanied by the sounds of birds and flowing water<ref name="ic"/>. The forest includes species such as {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Chamaecyparis obtusa}}, {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphor tree}}, {{ill|Oak|ja|カシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oak|1=Cyclobalanopsis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q796117}}, {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|en|Machilus thunbergii|WD=Q5792073|lt=Machilus thunbergii}}, and {{ill|Ilex latifolia|en|Ilex latifolia|WD=Q2706373|lt=Ilex latifolia}}<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is '''Mikoto no Mitama of Tsukuyomi''' (月読尊御魂)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity is also worshipped at the {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|1=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|2=ja|3=月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|ja|月夜見宮|1=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|2=ja|3=月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Gekū)|qq=}}, part of the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine<ref name="tm"/>. Although commonly regarded as a moon deity, the connection between the moon and water is emphasized here, with Tsukuyomi revered as a river deity at Kawahara Shrine<ref name="ic"/>.
Some theories suggest that both Sochi-cho, where Kawahara Shrine is located, and Nakamura-cho, home to Tsukuyomi-no-Miya, share historical ties through the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}<ref name="sk6"/>.
== History == According to legend, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was greeted here by Ōwakako no Mikoto (大若子命) before sailing down the Miyagawa River<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in 927 CE, which defines the auxiliary shrines of Ise<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. According to the ''Daijingū-shiki'', it was one of the shrines maintained by the Guji (宮司), receiving imperial offerings during the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} and {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}}<ref name="sk5"/>.
However, during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, ritual practices ceased, and the shrine fell into disrepair<ref name="tm"/>. The hereditary priests (祝部) also disappeared<ref name="sk6"/>.
In 1663 (Kanbun 3), {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} restored the shrine in Sochi<ref name="sk5"/>. A new shrine building was erected on October 9, 1663, and the deity was ceremonially transferred (遷御) on January 10, 1664<ref name="sk5"/>. Descendants of former priests were sought to resume their duties<ref name="sk6"/>. While local traditions guided the shrine's restoration, some suggest that the original site may have been {{ill|Takegahana Shrine|ja|竹鼻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takegahana Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Takegahana-cho or {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarabuchi Shrine|qq=}} in Funae<ref name="tm"/>.
Since the deity's transfer in 1694 (Genroku 7), the shrine has remained in its current location<ref name="sk5"/>. The shrine was rebuilt in 1908 (Meiji 41)<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.11</ref>, renovated in 1960 (Shōwa 35), and repaired again in 1983 (Shōwa 58)<ref name="sk6"/>.
== Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}}, and other special ceremonies, priests offer sacred food (御饌) and ritual offerings (幣帛) at the shrine<ref name="sk6"/>. For ceremonies like the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, remote rituals are conducted at the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Inner Shrine<ref name="sk6"/>.
== Access == The shrine is located within a grove just off {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.346–347</ref>.
* **By Bus:** Approximately a 12-minute walk (700 m) from the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū}} "Sochi" bus stop<ref name="ic"/>. From the bus stop, enter the alley opposite {{ill|Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|ja|伊勢市立佐八小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|1=Ise Municipal Sochi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379036}}, then follow the path along the rice fields to reach the shrine<ref name="ic"/>. * **By Train:** About 5.5 km from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}}, served by both {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}} {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line}}<ref name="sk5"/>.

enwiki== {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Aichi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2025}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} | image = Kawaharajinja2.JPG | caption = Kawahara Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|9|4.5|N|136|56|38.9|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Shōwa-ku]], [[Nagoya]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:Kawaharajinja1.JPG|thumb|''Haiden'' main hall]] The {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] and Buddhist [[Benten-dō]] located in [[Shōwa-ku]], [[Nagoya]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
The construction date of the shrine is not clear, it is assumed however that is appeared around 1000. The shrine underwent a number of reconstructions and renovations, the last one being in 1998. There is a pond with turtles that surround the shrine.
Since Buddhism and Shinto were closely intertwined, the shrine was also dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. Therefore, it is better known as the "Benten of Kawana" rather than "Kawahara Shrine".
The nearest [[Nagoya Municipal Bus]] stop is "Bentenmae" on the No. 18.
{{Shinto shrine}}

The construction date of the shrine is not clear, it is assumed however that is appeared around 1000. The shrine underwent a number of reconstructions and renovations, the last one being in 1998. There is a pond with turtles that surround the shrine.
Since Buddhism and Shinto were closely intertwined, the shrine was also dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. Therefore, it is better known as the "Benten of Kawana" rather than "Kawahara Shrine".
The nearest [[Nagoya Municipal Bus]] stop is "Bentenmae" on the No. 18.

==Merged content from enwiki== {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Aichi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2025}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} | image = Kawaharajinja2.JPG | caption = Kawahara Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|9|4.5|N|136|56|38.9|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Shōwa-ku]], [[Nagoya]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:Kawaharajinja1.JPG|thumb|''Haiden'' main hall]] The {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] and Buddhist [[Benten-dō]] located in [[Shōwa-ku]], [[Nagoya]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
The construction date of the shrine is not clear, it is assumed however that is appeared around 1000. The shrine underwent a number of reconstructions and renovations, the last one being in 1998. There is a pond with turtles that surround the shrine.
Since Buddhism and Shinto were closely intertwined, the shrine was also dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. Therefore, it is better known as the "Benten of Kawana" rather than "Kawahara Shrine".
The nearest [[Nagoya Municipal Bus]] stop is "Bentenmae" on the No. 18.

{{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} | image = Kawaharajinja2.JPG | caption = Kawahara Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|9|4.5|N|136|56|38.9|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Shōwa-ku]], [[Nagoya]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:Kawaharajinja1.JPG|thumb|''Haiden'' main hall]] The {{nihongo|'''Kawahara Shrine'''|川原神社|Kawahara-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] and Buddhist [[Benten-dō]] located in [[Shōwa-ku]], [[Nagoya]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
The construction date of the shrine is not clear, it is assumed however that is appeared around 1000. The shrine underwent a number of reconstructions and renovations, the last one being in 1998. There is a pond with turtles that surround the shrine.
Since Buddhism and Shinto were closely intertwined, the shrine was also dedicated to [[Benzaiten]]. Therefore, it is better known as the "Benten of Kawana" rather than "Kawahara Shrine".
The nearest [[Nagoya Municipal Bus]] stop is "Bentenmae" on the No. 18.
{{Shinto shrine}}
